items.headline: Age verification is the surveillance nobody voted for
items.subheadline: KOSA and Chat Control both retreated on their most-criticized measures this week, but both kept mandatory age verification, the quiet mechanism that turns anonymous browsing into identified browsing for all of us, argues Billions CEO Evin McMullen.
items.author: Evin McMullen

items.headline: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
items.subheadline: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.
items.author: Chet Shah

items.headline: The Clarity Act isn't a ticket to sanctions evasion, actually
items.subheadline: The bill, as currently drafted, has the power to stop sanctions evasion at scale, argues Ari Redbord, global head of policy at TRM Labs.
items.author: Ari Redbord

items.headline: The privacy paradox of protecting kids online
items.subheadline: We don’t need to imagine the privacy pitfalls of age verification. They’ve been happening for years, argues Cardano Foundation CEO Frederik Gregaard.
items.author: Frederik Gregaard

items.headline: Bitcoin, ether hold steady after rising on U.S. inflation report: Crypto Markets Today
items.subheadline: BTC rallied to a three-week high of $65,200 and ETH hit its highest since June 3 after a softer-than-expected inflation print eased fears over the U.S.-Iran conflict.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Bitcoin (BTC) price rally cools as investors digest inflation data, oil clouds outlook: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: The day ahead in crypto: July 15, 2026
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Strategy feels 'very secure' until bitcoin reaches $8,000-$10,000, says CEO
items.subheadline: Le highlighted increasing the U.S.-dollar reserve as an important lever in preferred stock STRC’s recovery back to around $90 having fallen below $75 last month.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Japan moves crypto under financial rules in regulatory overhaul
items.subheadline: The overhaul adds insider trading rules, tighter disclosures and tougher penalties while laying the groundwork for future bitcoin ETFs.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: How Robinhood Chain's biggest launchpad made $12 million and disappeared
items.subheadline: Noxa, the launchpad behind CASHCAT's rise, racked up nearly $12 million in fees before going dark, giving away its revenue, and leaving Robinhood Chain's memecoin economy in freefall.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin tops $65,000 as markets get more good inflation news
items.subheadline: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs took in about $181 million on Tuesday, a day after shedding roughly $425 million, per SoSoValue data. Ether ETFs added about $58 million.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Crypto’s security nightmare won’t be solved by ordinary audits
items.subheadline: Without an update to the current auditing infrastructure, the crypto space will likely continue to suffer significant losses, explains Beyer.
items.author: Stefan Beyer

items.headline: 'CASHCAT' trader turns $800 into over $1 million on Robinhood's brand new blockchain
items.subheadline: Robinhood's blockchain went live on July 1 to move stocks onchain. Its first breakout hit is a memecoin named after the mascot the company abandoned.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Crypto consolidates after selloff as South Korea's market meltdown drives crypto volumes higher
items.subheadline: Bitcoin consolidated after Monday's selloff as Trump threatened fresh Iran strikes, gold extended its slide and a KOSPI meltdown sent Korean crypto volumes soaring 1,426%.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Benchmark nearly doubles Hut 8 price target to $165 on Beacon Point AI data center deal
items.subheadline: Mark Palmer said Hut 8's Beacon Point project transforms the company into an AI infrastructure platform with a long growth runway.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: JPMorgan sees Hyperliquid partnership weighing on Circle, Coinbase
items.subheadline: Hyperliquid's deal with Circle and Coinbase creates a "prisoner's dilemma" that puts pressure on earnings from the dollar-pegged stablecoin.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Mizuho turns bearish on stablecoin issuer Circle, citing Open USD competition
items.subheadline: The Japanese investment bank said Open USD's yield pass-through model could pressure Circle's margins by shifting more reserve income to distributors.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Binance bets on becoming a crypto 'super app' as stablecoins reshape growth
items.subheadline: Shunyet Jan, Binance’s head of spot trading and derivatives, said the exchange is focusing more on payments and financial services than on trading alone as it drives the next phase of its expansion.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: UK plans first G7 digital sovereign bond by early 2027
items.subheadline: The security will launch on HSBC’s Orion platform within the BoE and FCA’s Digital Securities Sandbox to test reduced settlement times and costs.
items.author: Francisco Rodrigues

items.headline: Stripe, Advent mount a blockbuster $53 billion bid to buy PayPal (PYPL)
items.subheadline: Stripe and Advent’s bid follows an earlier expression of interest, and PayPal have been reluctant to engage with the offer thus far.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Bitcoin holds near $62,000 as RHODL compression signals the historic market rotation
items.subheadline: Long-term holders are quietly transferring supply to a new generation of buyers, but looming Federal Reserve rate hikes could still trigger the capitulation markets have been waiting for.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: U.S. June CPI fell 0.4%, likely cooling move toward Fed rate hikes
items.subheadline: This morning's report could go a long way toward determining whether the Federal Reserve raises interest rates at its late-July meeting.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: For pension funds, tokenization’s real play is balance sheet management, not just 24/7 liquidity, Fidelity’s Lai says
items.subheadline: Fidelity International’s Giselle Lai argues that the most compelling long-term use case for tokenized funds is balance sheet management for large, global institutions, rather than just 24/7 liquidity.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: BTC, ETH, SOL price news: Bitcoin nears $65,000 as Fed rate-hike expectations drop
items.subheadline: The June CPI print  pulled hike odds from 43% to 13%, with analysts now watching the September FOMC meeting for further cues on positioning.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum's newest nonprofit wants to become Wall Street's guide to crypto
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re digging into Ethereum Institutional, a new nonprofit aimed at educating financial institutions and banks about Ethereum.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Live updates: Bitcoin takes aim at $65,000, erasing losses from earlier this week
items.subheadline: Spot bitcoin funds lost about $95 million on Thursday and ether funds roughly $52 million, ending the one bright spot in crypto's institutional flows even as prices rallied.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: ETH news: Ethereum Foundation says AI found bug that could take validators offline
items.subheadline: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: BTC news: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
items.subheadline: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Michael Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Robinhood Chain scores strong debut, Bernstein says
items.subheadline: Robinhood's new blockchain quickly became a top-five network by DEX volume, signaling strong early demand for its tokenized asset platform, the broker said.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Robinhood's blockchain finds early success — Thanks to memecoins, not stocks
items.subheadline: Robinhood Chain has pulled in $135 million of value and 800,000 addresses since July 1. Almost none of it is doing what the chain was built for.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin's BIP-110 sparked a fight over who gets to decide the future of Bitcoin
items.subheadline: BIP-110 sought to limit non-financial data on Bitcoin's blockchain but instead reignited debates over censorship and decentralization.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) Foundation spinout EthSystems targets banks with blockchain privacy technology
items.subheadline: The spinout comes amid one of the biggest organizational shakeups at the Ethereum Foundation in years.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Ripple joins card giants backing x402 as 75 million payments move just $24 million
items.subheadline: Forty companies now govern x402, the protocol Coinbase built and handed away. It settled about $24 million across 75 million payments last month.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: The SEC delayed tokenizing stocks, and here’s why that’s a relief
items.subheadline: Before we rush to tokenize U.S. equities, we need compliance systems that understand context, not just checkboxes. Investors like Michael Burry see the danger, so should the rest of us, urges Chamarajnagar.
items.author: Dr. Ravishankar Chamarajnagar

items.headline: The banking lobby is wrong about stablecoins and community banks
items.subheadline: Congress should not kneecap one of the clearest advances in payment infrastructure to protect community banks from a threat that has not been proven, argues Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.
items.author: Ryne Sax

items.headline: What Robinhood’s recent layoffs say about the current state of crypto investments
items.subheadline: Despite the trading platform’s restructuring and the wave of crypto companies making cuts, signaling a late bear market, Altcoin Pro’s Horst, Anderson and Zhuleku explain there’s no reason to panic.
items.author: Ryan Horst

items.headline: Tokenized securities need competition, not gatekeepers
items.subheadline: Innovation thrives when investors have choices. For tokenized securities, Washington shouldn't pick winners before the market has a chance to learn what works, argues Patrick McHenry, vice chairman at Ondo Finance and former Chairman of the House Financial Services Committee.
items.author: Patrick McHenry

items.headline: Europe is closing the door on offshore crypto, but it’s leaving the riskiest window open
items.subheadline: MiCA was never meant to address the giant crypto derivatives market. That could pose a serious problem, says Patrick Gruhn, founder and chief executive of Perpetuals.com.
items.author: Patrick Gruhn

items.headline: Europe led on crypto regulation. Now implementation must match ambition
items.author: Richard Teng

items.headline: Collateral, not yield, will decide which stablecoins win
items.subheadline: As yield-bearing stablecoins race toward a $50 billion market capitalization, the industry is optimizing for the wrong metric, argues Artem Tolkachev, chief RWA officer at Falcon Finance.
items.author: Artem Tolkachev

items.headline: U.S. government digital dollar set to be banned tonight under housing law's CBDC limit
items.subheadline: Despite President Donald Trump's refusal to sign Congress' bipartisan housing bill, it's set to go into effect at midnight, and its temporary CBDC ban along with it.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Signs of life?: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: Several sources told CoinDesk that a new draft of the Clarity Act may drop this week, but challenges remain.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: Battle over blockchain stock ownership is heading to Washington regulators
items.subheadline: The Securities Transfer Association, an industry group for transfer agents, said company-authorized tokenization should receive preferential treatment under future rules.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Ripple news: XRP-linked firm lands inside UK plan for tokenized repo, bonds and funds
items.subheadline: A Treasury-backed report targets putting repo, gilts and funds onchain within two years, and warms to permissionless networks that firms like Ripple have been building on.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Trump's crypto riches loom over Clarity Act talks to ban conflicts for U.S. officials
items.subheadline: Discussion among Democrats have fixed on the president's personal gains when hashing out the crypto market structure bill's ethics provision.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S., UK move to align rules for tokenized finance across world's largest financial markets
items.subheadline: The joint roadmap aims to reduce regulatory friction for tokenized assets while strengthening ties between the world's two largest financial centers.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Why multi-billion dollar crypto networks are missing from Wikipedia
items.subheadline: Wikipedia observes guidelines on cryto coverage which date back many years, and may now be outdated, according to the report.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Some U.S. Senate Democrats come out against Clarity Act, calling it a 'corrupt' bill
items.subheadline: Certain Democrats are taking an increasingly rigorous stand against the crypto market structure bill, which needs a significant number from their party to pass.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S. CFTC moves to stop Kalshi from canceling trades as ordered by Michigan court
items.subheadline: The federal derivatives regulator, which oversees trading at Kalshi, argued it was inappropriate for Michigan to "bully" the firm into reversing trades.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: BlackRock's crypto assets fall 39% despite $15 billion of net inflows
items.subheadline: BlackRock's digital asset funds attracted $15 billion in net inflows over the past year, but falling crypto prices drove the value of those holdings sharply lower.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news): A timeline of the Ethereum Foundation's ongoing shakeup
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we’re unpacking the timeline of all the changes at the Ethereum Foundation this year.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Open USD poses new threat to Circle by challenging USDC’s core business model, CoinShares says
items.subheadline: The consortium-backed stablecoin would share reserve income with partners instead of the issuer, pressuring Circle's margins if it debuts in 2026.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: South Korea’s new economic roadmap is a massive bet on blockchain technology
items.subheadline: From tokenized public spending trials to government bond pilots, the state is aggressively pushing public finance onto distributed ledgers.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Clarity Act negotiations: High-level White House meeting planned, sources say
items.subheadline: The most contentious piece of the crypto market structure bill is unresolved in the final weeks of Senate runway, and the president is expected to discuss it.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Ostium loses $18 million in oracle attack that gamed its own price-feed infrastructure
items.subheadline: A hacker used Ostium's own price-reporting infrastructure against the protocol, submitting falsified future-dated oracle data to manufacture fake trading profits and trigger an $18 million payout.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Cantor and Securitize collaborate on blockchain-based IPOs
items.subheadline: The move creates a pathway for public companies to raise capital onchain and issue tokenized securities.
items.author: Ian Allison

items.headline: DTCC moves tokenized securities into live trading, marking a milestone for Wall Street's blockchain push
items.subheadline: DTCC's first live production trades using tokenized securities show how blockchain could reshape the infrastructure behind Wall Street.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Base creator Jesse Pollak pivots after admitting social strategy failed
items.subheadline: Pollak said that Jordan Fish, also known on X as 'Cobie,' will lead the Base app team.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Two groups of BTC investors sell on the rise as prices near $65,000.
items.subheadline: Two distinct groups of on-chain holders are selling into BTC’s price bounce.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: ETH news: Ethereum outruns bitcoin as ETF money returns, almost all of it from BlackRock's fund
items.subheadline: This is not a broad rally as bitcoin is up 4% over the same stretch, and solana, TRON and hyperliquid are all lower.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: A bitcoin wallet dormant since the 2017 peak just moved $383 million
items.subheadline: The coins went to a fresh address rather than an exchange, so nothing has been sold yet.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: U.S. Senate unanimously opposes clemency for FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried
items.subheadline: The nonbinding resolution passed without objection after Bankman-Fried asked for clemency, months after Trump pardoned other major crypto figures, including Changpeng Zhao and Ross Ulbricht.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: ZachXBT calls hardware wallets complete garbage; BTC steady near $65,000
items.subheadline: Blockchain investigator ZachXBT came down heavily on hardware wallets. BTC traded steady near $65,000 after South Korea's rate hike.
items.author: Omkar Godbole
